slides.lead: Demo (sample) presentation template. All text is dummy placeholder content and only serves to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. This is where the event name, a one-line subtitle and the bottom meta row belong. Highlight a word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared mission: more smiles.
slides.notes: Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three big tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: The kids, the parents and the moments that matter most.
slides.notes: Section divider between blocks. Big headline on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: A toy is never just plastic and paint. It is a story a child tells themselves, again and again.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: big headline, bold illustration, short caption. Great for a key point or a chapter.

items: of parents say our toys hold attention past the first week
average focused play time per session at home
repeat purchase rate across the core range
bullets: Measure delight, not shelf space: play time beats packaging.
Start where the joy is real and the demand already shows.
A small loved range beats a big forgettable catalog.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: A toy earns its place through what it invites, not what it does.
bullets: Open-ended play with no single right answer.
New layers as the child gets older and bolder.
Moments shared with a sibling, friend or parent.
Built to survive the floor, the bath and the years.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself up.

slides.lead: Same shelf, two philosophies and two very different afterlives.
cards: Sells fast on a fad, forgotten by spring.
Rides a known character, fades when the show ends.
Earns its place over years of open play.
Outlives childhood and gets handed down.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six building blocks behind a toy kids return to.
cards: Tested, non-toxic and gentle on small hands.
Tuned to what the child can do and want next.
The first ten seconds that win a child over.
Designed for more than one pair of hands.
Drops, chews and washes without giving up.
Packaging a parent understands in one glance.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Sales settle into a calm, dependable rhythm.
Word of mouth spreads and demand climbs fast.
A fad cools and shelf space is better spent elsewhere.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a title and a small caption.

slides.quote: A child does not remember the toy that did everything. They remember the one that let them do everything.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget on a deadline.
bullets: Each toy promises one thing and delivers it well.
Real children decide, not the boardroom.
Nothing ships until it passes every check.
slides.notes: A plain bulleted slide with a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A toy crammed with buttons but no story ends up under the bed.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this season.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the table and open a fresh set live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered headline, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
